reasoning: PKGBUILD uses verified HTTPS sources with pinned checksums and standard packaging patterns. Qwen's supply-chain concerns are over-cautious for legitimate CDN usage.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misinterpreted CrabNebula CDN as untrusted despite checksum verification and upstream association, while DeepSeek correctly contextualized the workflow.
coverage_issues: Qwen skipped critical files: aur-release.sh (release automation) and wrapper script in PKGBUILD
false_positives: Qwen's 'untrusted CDN' alert: False positive. cdn.crabnebula.app is legitimate upstream infrastructure with SHA256 verification.
Qwen's 'wrapper script risk': False positive. Heredoc is standard for binary launchers with documented GPU fallback logic.
learnings: Prioritize reviewing: 1) All executable scripts referenced in PKGBUILD, 2) Binary wrapper logic, 3) Maintainer automation scripts
Improve model training: Distinguish between legitimate CDNs vs truly suspicious sources; recognize standard binary packaging patterns
Fix failure mode: Reduce false alarms on verified third-party infrastructure common in -bin packages
